title: Enforce pre-phase ownership on active routing mutations
action: At the strict generic state mutation boundary, require an explicit --owner equal to the selected default or lane record's valid pre-mutation phase for every state set mutation of phase, mode, feature, summary, or next_action (D4, D6). Reject missing or mismatched owner before any write while preserving existing transition and compounding-tail refusal precedence. Add the option to the command registry and remediation text; migrate the real exploring, planning, validating, and compounding callers to their pre-phases. Remove the independent-review summary state-set caller rather than granting review an owner, and keep review outcomes in the review session only (D4, D5). Apply the same implementation to the vendored runtime mirrors, preserve unrelated dirty hunks, and add behavior tests for default/lane parity, phase-change ownership rollover, corrupt state, refusal byte identity, gate-command separation, caller census, and review isolation.

truths: A generic routing mutation without --owner refuses and leaves the selected state bytes unchanged.
A mismatched owner refuses identically for default state and lanes; a matching pre-phase owner changes only the selected record.
After a legal phase change, the previous owner cannot authorize the next mutation.
No owner field is persisted and legacy valid state/lane JSON remains readable.
state gate keeps its existing approval and bypass contract and does not accept routing ownership.
Independent review has no canonical instruction that mutates active routing state, and review records never decide execution readiness.

artifacts.substantive: enforces owner before generic state mutation using strict selected-record state
documents and validates the required owner option
covers authority, zero-write refusal, caller census, and default/lane behavior
records review-local UAT/reasons without generic active-state mutation
runtime mirror is behaviorally and byte aligned with canonical CLI

key_links: handleStateSet reads the selected record phase before comparing --owner and before constructing any write.
Command registry schema, CLI handler, examples, guards, and every live skill caller use the same owner vocabulary.
Review doctrine routes status and UAT reasons through bee reviews record while preserving active state bytes.
prohibitions: Do not persist an owner field or add a state migration.
Do not give independent review a reviewing owner.
Do not weaken strict reads, atomic writes, transition guards, compounding tail guards, or gate approval semantics.
Do not rewrite unrelated pre-existing hunks in test_lib or routing doctrine.
Do not alter the corrected worktree-isolation default route.

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && git diff --check

trace.outcome: Generic routing mutations now enforce selected-record pre-phase ownership; shipped callers and review isolation are migrated and verified.
